audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra offering text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for call from tent, whole burnt offering/olah from herd, flock, or birds, hand-laying, blood/life, altar, and soothing scent.

verses.restored: YHWH called to Moses and spoke to him from tent of meeting, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: When a human from you brings near an offering to YHWH, from beast, from herd or from flock, you shall bring near your offering.
If his offering is burnt offering from herd, a complete male he shall bring near; to entrance of tent of meeting he shall bring it near for acceptance before YHWH.
He shall lay his hand upon head of burnt offering, and it shall be accepted for him to make atonement/covering for him.
He shall slaughter son of cattle before YHWH; sons of Aaron, the priests, shall bring near blood/life and throw blood/life against altar all around, at entrance of tent of meeting.
He shall skin burnt offering and cut it into its pieces.
Sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on altar and arrange wood upon fire.
Sons of Aaron, the priests, shall arrange pieces, head, and fat upon wood that is on fire upon altar.
Its inward parts and legs he shall wash with water, and priest shall turn all into smoke on altar; it is burnt offering, fire-offering, soothing scent to YHWH.
If his offering is from flock, from sheep or goats, for burnt offering, complete male he shall bring near.
He shall slaughter it on north side of altar before YHWH, and sons of Aaron, the priests, shall throw its blood/life against altar all around.
He shall cut it into its pieces, with its head and fat, and priest shall arrange them on wood that is on fire upon altar.
Inward parts and legs he shall wash with water, and priest shall bring near all and turn it into smoke on altar; it is burnt offering, fire-offering, soothing scent to YHWH.
If his offering to YHWH is burnt offering from birds, he shall bring near his offering from turtledoves or sons of pigeon.
The priest shall bring it near to altar, pinch off its head, turn it into smoke on altar, and its blood/life shall be drained on side of altar.
He shall remove its crop with its feathers/waste and throw it beside altar eastward, to place of ashes.
He shall tear it open by its wings, not divide it completely; priest shall turn it into smoke on altar, on wood that is on fire; it is burnt offering, fire-offering, soothing scent to YHWH.

translation_notes: Hebrew Vayiqra opens with calling from the completed tent: Leviticus continues Exodus presence.
Korban/offering means something brought near; sacrificial grammar is about approach/nearness, not crude appeasement.
Olah/burnt offering rises wholly in smoke; preserve as whole-offering/ascending offering in later audit.
Blood/life language should be kept as ritual life-symbolism, not gore spectacle.
Bird option preserves access for poorer worshipers.
safety_note: Do not read Leviticus 1 as Divine hunger for violence. It is ancient ritual grammar for approach, surrender, and life-symbolism around the altar, with scaled access for different means.
